skill.name: gwas-database
skill.description: Query the NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog for SNP-trait associations, studies, variants, traits, p-values, effect sizes, and summary statistics.
skill.summary: Use NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog data to investigate SNP, trait, gene, and study associations.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks, URLs, and GWAS scientific notation as high-risk patterns, but these are largely documentation false positives. The real risks are legitimate public network access, an FTP/wget download example, and a local file write example for summary statistics, so publication is acceptable with a network and download warning.

medium_findings.title: Public Network Requests to GWAS Catalog APIs
medium_findings.description: The skill documents Python requests calls to NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog endpoints. This is expected for the skill, but it sends user-selected query terms, variant IDs, and trait IDs to an external public service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are explicit and repeated, and the destination is the official GWAS Catalog domain. I did not find evidence that credentials or local secrets are collected or transmitted.

medium_findings.title: Bulk Summary Statistics Download Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill includes examples for downloading GWAS summary statistics with FTP or streamed HTTP. This can retrieve large files and should be run only when the user expects external downloads.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The download behavior is directly shown in documentation examples. It targets EBI public data, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Local File Write in Download Helper Example
low_findings.description: The reference documentation writes a downloaded compressed summary statistics file to a user-provided output directory. The pattern is expected for a downloader, but users should choose output paths deliberately.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write is explicit and limited to saving downloaded data. I did not find evidence of overwriting sensitive paths or writing executable content.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick and Weak-Crypto Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Many external command and weak cryptography alerts point to Markdown inline code, code fences, REST terminology, GWAS names, endpoint text, p-value notation, or HTTP status documentation rather than executable risky code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows Markdown documentation and examples, not Ruby execution or cryptographic implementation. No executable skill script is present in the scanned files.

dangerous_patterns.title: HTTP Client Calls in Example Code
dangerous_patterns.description: Python requests.get examples contact public GWAS Catalog services. This pattern is necessary for the skill but should be disclosed because query values leave the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is clearly present and semantically aligned with the skill purpose. The destination is an official public data service, not an unknown endpoint.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Download Command Example
dangerous_patterns.description: A wget command over FTP is documented for bulk summary statistics retrieval. This is a user-directed command example, not hidden execution by the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell command is visible in a workflow section and uses the EBI public FTP path. There is no evidence that the skill executes it automatically.

content.value_statement: Genetic association searches often require careful endpoint selection and result interpretation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through GWAS Catalog queries for variants, traits, genes, studies, and summary statistics.
actual_capabilities: Explains GWAS Catalog entities, including studies, associations, variants, and EFO traits.
Shows how to query variants by rs ID and retrieve associated traits and p-values.
Guides trait and disease searches using EFO identifiers and GWAS Catalog endpoints.
Provides examples for study metadata, publication lookup, pagination, and response handling.
Documents summary statistics access for chromosome regions, traits, and bulk study downloads.
Supports research workflows for systematic reviews, gene interpretation, and polygenic risk score preparation.
limitations: It depends on public GWAS Catalog API availability and current endpoint behavior.
It does not validate clinical actionability or provide medical advice.
Large summary statistics downloads may require storage, bandwidth, and manual review.
It provides guidance and examples, not a packaged executable client or database mirror.

use_cases.title: Variant Association Lookup
use_cases.description: Find reported traits, p-values, effect sizes, and study links for a specific rs ID.

use_cases.title: Trait Evidence Review
use_cases.description: Collect genome-wide significant associations for a disease or phenotype using EFO terms and pagination.

use_cases.title: PRS Variant Preparation
use_cases.description: Identify candidate variants and summary statistics sources for polygenic risk score exploration.

prompt_templates.title: Look Up a Variant
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the GWAS Catalog skill to look up rs7903146. Summarize associated traits, strongest p-values, reported alleles, and key study identifiers.

prompt_templates.title: Search a Trait
prompt_templates.prompt: Find GWAS Catalog associations for type 2 diabetes. Use the relevant EFO trait when possible and return variants, p-values, effect sizes, and PubMed IDs.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Gene Region Evidence
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of variants near a gene.
prompt_templates.prompt: Investigate GWAS evidence near APOE. Include associated traits, genomic context, study metadata, and notes on ancestry or cohort information.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Summary Statistics Retrieval
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reproducible workflow to retrieve GWAS summary statistics for a selected GCST study. Include endpoint choices, pagination, download checks, and storage cautions.

output_examples.input: Look up rs7903146 in the GWAS Catalog.
output_examples.output: A concise variant report with associated traits, p-value ranges, reported risk alleles, study accessions, PubMed identifiers, and links to relevant GWAS Catalog records.

output_examples.input: Find significant associations for type 2 diabetes.
output: Trait search strategy using the EFO identifier
Table-style summary of variants, genes, p-values, and effect measures
Notes on pagination, ancestry fields, and evidence limits

output_examples.input: Prepare to download summary statistics for a GCST accession.
output_examples.output: A download plan that identifies the summary statistics endpoint, expected file type, local storage needs, validation checks, and respectful API usage guidance.

best_practices: Use stable identifiers such as rs IDs, GCST accessions, PubMed IDs, and EFO terms when available.
Apply pagination, delays, and caching when collecting many GWAS Catalog records.
Verify p-values, effect directions, ancestry context, and study metadata before downstream interpretation.
anti_patterns: Do not treat GWAS associations as clinical diagnosis or treatment guidance.
Do not download bulk summary statistics without confirming storage, licensing, and analysis needs.
Do not merge studies or traits without checking ancestry, phenotype definitions, and genome build details.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: No. The documented GWAS Catalog APIs are public and do not require authentication.

faq.question: Can it search by SNP rs ID?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains variant lookup endpoints and how to retrieve trait associations for an rs ID.

faq.question: Can it retrieve full summary statistics?
faq.answer: It documents summary statistics API and bulk download patterns when data are available for a study.

faq.question: Does it provide medical advice?
faq.answer: No. It supports research interpretation of public GWAS data and should not guide clinical decisions.

faq.question: What data sources does it use?
faq.answer: It focuses on the NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog REST API and Summary Statistics API.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill includes network requests, an FTP download example, and a local file write example for downloaded data.
